UserComments.Comment: Charging from EVN's Mennekes sockets: Do not exceed 3hrs.of continuous charging to avoid extra fees. To fully charge your battery from Mennekes socket restart charging every 3hrs. Town hall's Schuko sockets: Free,no authentication, unlimited time. Town hall's Mennekes/400V socket: Reserved for town hall's vehicle,not public,do not use.I was charging from 1 of the Schuko socket,this is 1 of the few places, where U can charge @ 16A from a free Schuko socket, as my vehicle has 1-phase onboard charger,Schuko sockets r my favorite. I used them even before I knew, these r free,because I always wished to let Mennekes sockets free for crews of newer vehicles with 3-phase onboard charger, so when I find a Schuko socket, I am using it letting Mennekes free for those,who can use 3-phase charging & who has less time for charging. This street has no direct connection with main avenue, U must drive around a block or 2 via backstreets,but this is the cheapest charging hub in town(the only with free charging option),for e-bikes,e-scooters etc.,it can also be accessed from the main avenue via sidewalk (I do not advise to drive on/via sidewalk, I advise e-bike/e-scooter drivers to tow their vehicle from road to Schuko sockets via sidewalk, or they may also drive around by back streets like car drivers).Enjoy.Live long & prosper.

AddressInfo.Title: EVN Public Charging Station + Town Hall's Sockets.
AddressInfo.AccessComments: Unlimited public access: from right to left:3 public parking places for EVs;1 reserved public place for town hall's EV,the left-most Mennekes(out of public sockets count) is reserved for town hall's EV.

Connections.Reference: EVN.at (2 places @ right side-right on arrival).

Connections.Comments: Free of charge, on left side(left on arrival).

GeneralComments: From right to left(view on arrival): 2xMennekes-RFID authentication-payed by EVN tariff; 3x Schuko: Free of charge;1x 3phase/Mennekes reserved for town hall's car.
